mode.title: Mode
mode.summary: How clips are combined before moshing.
mode.effect: Single clip strips I-frames from one video. Splice two clips uses the anchor clip's first I-frame plus the motion clip's P-frames. Datamosh transition plays video 1 clean, a cross-clip smear bridge, then video 2 clean.

source.title: Motion clip
source.summary: The clip whose P-frames drive the smear.
source.effect: In single-clip mode this is the only input. In splice mode its P-frames are concatenated after the anchor header, so movement and color from this clip bleed through the frozen anchor image.

reference.title: Anchor clip
reference.summary: The still-image base for two-clip splice.
reference.effect: Only the first I-frame (plus SPS/PPS headers) from this clip is kept. The decoder treats it as the base image; motion-clip P-frames then update that stale reference, creating cross-video glitch trails.

mosh_start_seconds.title: Mosh / transition start
mosh_start_seconds.summary: When the effect begins — on the source clip, or on video 1 in transition mode.
mosh_start_seconds.effect: Single clip: footage before this stays normal. Transition mode: video 1 plays clean until this point, then the datamosh bridge begins using this frame as the pixel anchor.

transition_duration_seconds.title: Transition length
transition_duration_seconds.summary: How long the datamosh bridge between video 1 and video 2 lasts.
transition_duration_seconds.effect: Video 2's P-frames drive the smear for this duration while decoding against video 1's frame at the cut point. With prep enabled, keyframes are forced at the cut and at the end of this window so the bridge length matches the slider and video 2 resumes cleanly afterward.

mosh_end_seconds.title: Mosh end
mosh_end_seconds.summary: When the datamosh effect stops, in seconds from the start of the source clip.
mosh_end_seconds.effect: Footage after this time returns to normal with all keyframes kept. Set to 0 (or leave at end of clip) to mosh through the end. Must be after Mosh start. Combine both for a glitch burst in the middle of a clip.

gop.title: GOP length
gop.summary: Keyframe interval used during prep re-encode.
gop.effect: GOP = Group of Pictures. Higher values mean fewer I-frames in the prepared stream, so more P-frames depend on a single reference — stronger, longer smears. Lower values add more reset points and reduce mosh intensity. Only applies when prep re-encode is enabled.

duplicate_copies.title: P-frame duplicates
duplicate_copies.summary: How many extra copies of each P-frame are inserted.
duplicate_copies.effect: Each duplicate reuses the same compressed P-frame data, stretching motion trails and stuttering movement. 0 = off. Values of 2–4 produce obvious temporal smear; higher values can make the clip much longer and more chaotic.

duplicate_probability.title: Duplicate chance
duplicate_probability.summary: Probability that a given P-frame gets duplicated.
duplicate_probability.effect: At 100% every eligible P-frame is duplicated. Lower values randomize the effect — some frames smear, others play normally — for a more broken, uneven glitch look. Use with Random seed for repeatable results.

keep_first_idr.title: Keep first IDR frames
keep_first_idr.summary: Number of I-frames (IDR slices) to preserve in the output.
keep_first_idr.effect: IDR frames reset the decoder. Removing all of them (0) breaks MP4 remux — ffmpeg writes audio only and players show a black screen. Keep at least 1 for playable MP4 output. Higher values leave more reset points and shorten smears.

width.title: Output width
width.effect: Re-encodes the video to this width (height scales to keep aspect ratio). Smaller widths process faster and can exaggerate blockiness. Leave empty to keep the source resolution.

crf.title: CRF
crf.summary: Constant Rate Factor — prep encode quality.
crf.effect: Lower CRF = higher quality, larger files, sharper blocks in glitches. Higher CRF = more compression artifacts that blend into the mosh. Default 18 is visually lossless-ish; try 22–28 for a grittier look.

seed.title: Random seed
seed.effect: When duplicate chance is below 100%, the same seed reproduces the same pattern of duplicated frames across runs. Leave empty for a different random pattern each time.

remove_sps_pps.title: Remove SPS/PPS
remove_sps_pps.summary: Strip sequence/picture parameter sets from the bitstream.
remove_sps_pps.effect: SPS and PPS tell the decoder how to interpret the stream. Removing them can cause harsher decode errors and more extreme corruption. Single-clip mode only — can make output unplayable in some players.

no_prep.title: Skip prep re-encode
no_prep.summary: Copy the existing H.264 stream instead of re-encoding.
no_prep.effect: Faster, but only works well if the source already has long GOPs and no B-frames. Videos with frequent keyframes or B-frames will mosh weakly or unpredictably. In transition mode, prep also adds mid-clip keyframes on video 2 so the clean ending can start — disabling this often causes transition length errors.

no_audio.title: Drop audio
no_audio.summary: Exclude audio from the output file.
no_audio.effect: Video-only MP4. Useful when you only need the visual glitch or plan to add sound elsewhere. When off, audio is copied from the source clip.
